title: Strictly causal lap-history features per driver-race-stint
summary: The current best code is almost entirely row-local.
rationale: The current best code is almost entirely row-local. Recent F1 modeling work treats tyre pace/degradation as a latent evolving process, and generic time-series validation guidance warns that adjacent observations are highly autocorrelated. A compact causal history block could capture the 'pit next lap' trigger better than more static heuristics.
implementation_hint: Sort by Year/Race/Driver/LapNumber and build only backward-looking features: lag1/lag2/lag3 of LapTime_Delta, Cumulative_Degradation, Position_Change; rolling mean/std/slope over last 3-5 laps; laps since last pit; EWMA pace-loss slope within stint. Validate with folds that never let future laps from the same race leak backward.
expected_effect: Should improve recall on imminent-stop rows where pace falls off abruptly, with better LB alignment than static tyre-window tweaks.
risk: Easy to leak if lags/rolls are computed before splitting or if folds mix neighboring laps from the same race/driver.
